notes: 1959 brick block in Wola, post-renovation, ground floor, spółdzielcze własnościowe ownership form, czynsz 930 PLN (12.7 PLN/m² — within the normal 8–15 PLN/m² band, so we DON'T require it in §6 risks; the agent rightly treats it as an expense in §5, not a structural risk). Asking 1.29M PLN at 17,671 PLN/m². The reference case we've iterated on throughout development. Yield band 4.5–7.0 — widened from 5.0 after the red-team step pushed a more conservative rent for this ground-floor older block (recorded 4.65%, vs 5.35% pre-red-team).

Central-premium tier: a 42 m² 2-room flat in a 1930 pre-war kamienica on ul. Żurawia, Śródmieście, marketed for investment. Asking 795,000 PLN (18,929 PLN/m²), priced below the comp median. Sourced for central-district and pre-war-build coverage. Recorded Buy at 6.21% after the §8 verdict-calibration change — a solid in-range yield at a genuine discount, the cleanest Buy in the suite. Yield band 5.1–6.8 brackets the comp p25–p75. Risk substrings: the elevated czynsz (20.2 PLN/m²) and the below-comp discount.

Outer tier with the suite's heaviest supply pipeline: a 53 m² 3-room 2025 new-build, top floor (6/6), on ul. Instalatorów in Raków, Włochy. (Sourced under a working 'Ochota' label that was wrong — the listing is Włochy/Raków; the case id and notes are corrected here.) Asking 1,060,000 PLN (19,996 PLN/m²), ~21% above the comp median. Sourced for new-build and extreme-supply coverage (Włochy 52.3 completions/1,000 residents). Yield band 4.2–5.7 brackets the comp p25–p75; the agent chose the p75 rent benchmark, so the recorded 5.38% sits near the top. Risk substrings: price premium and supply pipeline.

Transit-strong outer-inner tier: a 62 m² 3-room flat in a 2008 block on ul. Górczewska, Bemowo, ~400 m from the M2 metro. Asking 979,000 PLN (15,911 PLN/m²) — fair vs comps, but czynsz of 1,400 PLN (22.7 PLN/m²) is the heaviest in the suite and drags net yield to ~3.3%. Sourced as a metro-proximity contrast to the outer cases. Yield band 4.3–5.6 brackets the comp p25–p75 (≈4.5–5.4%). Risk substrings: the extreme czynsz and Bemowo's heavy new-build supply.

Outer tier, private seller: a 46 m² 2-room flat in a 2018 MintCity block on ul. Budowlana, Bródno, Targówek. Asking 860,000 PLN (18,655 PLN/m²), ~31% above the comp median — the steepest premium in the suite. Carries an active mortgage to be discharged at sale. Sourced for outer-district and private-seller coverage. Yield band 3.8–5.0 brackets the comp p25–p75; the agent chose the p75 rent benchmark, so the recorded 4.70% sits at the top. Risk substrings: price premium and the mortgage encumbrance.

Inner-ring tier — a 48 m² 2-room flat near Plac Unii, central Mokotów, asking 790,000 PLN (16,434 PLN/m²). Recorded Borderline at 6.28% gross yield: a good yield offset by high czynsz and renovation capex. Sourced for Mokotów coverage and a mid-price central reference.

Inner-ring tier — an 85 m² 4-room family flat in Mokotów (Służewiec), an off-plan unit, asking 1,385,000 PLN (16,315 PLN/m²). Recorded Borderline (leaning Walk) at 6.1% — a transit-poor location and an implausibly low quoted czynsz undercut the headline yield. Sourced for large-flat (4-room) size coverage.

Inner-ring tier — a 50 m² 2-room top-floor flat in a 1938 pre-war kamienica in Stara Ochota, asking 920,000 PLN (18,218 PLN/m²), priced at the comp median. Sourced for Ochota coverage and a second pre-war-build case. Verdict has walked across iterations: Buy at 5.87% (§8 calibration) → Borderline at 4.96% (red-team) → Walk at 4.70% (heuristic-priors). Yield below 5% with extreme czynsz now reads honestly as Walk; band updated to ['Walk','Borderline'] reflecting the realistic range. Risk substrings: the elevated czynsz (16.8 PLN/m²) and top-floor roof exposure.

Inner-ring tier — a 27 m² studio (kawalerka) in Ochota, to-renovate, asking 648,000 PLN (23,981 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 4.82% — an 18% price premium plus renovation capex. Sourced for the smallest-size segment (1-room studio). sale_comp_count_min lowered to 1 (the 1–2 room / 22–32 m² Ochota sale segment is thin — only 4 raw comps in the snapshot, 2 unique after dedup; the agent honestly falls back to district-wide stats).

Central-premium tier — a 69 m² 3-room top-floor flat in Żoliborz Artystyczny, private seller, asking 1,900,000 PLN (27,484 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 4.02% — a 24.8% price premium and the suite's lowest gross yield. Sourced for Żoliborz coverage and a premium top-floor case.

Central-premium tier — a 52 m² 2-room ground-floor flat with a garden in Żoliborz, asking 1,115,000 PLN (21,360 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 4.38% — a heavy czynsz and a ground-floor position that the below-comp price reflects rather than discounts. Sourced for a second Żoliborz profile. Risk substrings: czynsz and 'ground' (matches the agent's ground-floor risk regardless of hyphenation; the 'discount' framing drifted out of §7 after the red-team pass).

Outer tier, sourced as a Buy candidate — a 57 m² 3-room top-floor flat in Bielany, asking 680,000 PLN (11,972 PLN/m²), ~28% below the comp median. Recorded Walk at 6.62% (the suite's highest gross yield): the agent treats the renovation budget and the limited (spółdzielcze) ownership as outweighing the headline yield. Sourced to probe whether a high yield alone triggers a Buy — it does not.

Outer tier — a 67 m² 3-room apartament with three terraces in Bielany, asking 1,449,000 PLN (21,753 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 4.14% — a 32% price premium and a high czynsz. Sourced as a premium intra-district contrast to bielany-panorama.

Outer tier — a 48 m² 2-room flat with air-conditioning in Ursus, 2020 build, asking 799,000 PLN (16,691 PLN/m²). Recorded Borderline at 5.26% — a high-quality unit priced at the district p75, with extreme local new-build supply. Sourced for Ursus coverage.

Outer tier — a 55 m² 3-room off-plan flat in Ursus, asking 826,000 PLN (15,120 PLN/m²). Recorded Borderline (leaning Walk) at 5.8% — an undisclosed czynsz and extreme local supply. Sourced for a second Ursus profile in a cheap western district.

Outer tier, sourced as a Buy candidate — a 62 m² modern 3-room flat with a garden in Ursynów (Jeziorki), an off-plan unit, asking 790,000 PLN (12,742 PLN/m²), below the district sale p25. Recorded Walk at 5.09% — developer/delivery risk, no metro and an undisclosed czynsz. The cheap entry price did not translate into a Buy.

Outer tier, private seller — a 54 m² 2-room flat with a garage by Metro Natolin, Ursynów, asking 1,098,000 PLN (20,253 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 4.37% — a 19% price premium despite the metro proximity. Sourced as a premium intra-district contrast to ursynow-ogrodek.

Outer-premium tier — a 78 m² 3-room flat (1996, un-renovated) with a garage in Wilanów, asking 1,350,000 PLN (17,386 PLN/m²), ~15.7% below the comp median. Recorded Borderline at 5.16% — a real discount that mostly reflects the renovation need. Sourced for Wilanów coverage.

Outer-premium tier — an 84 m² 3-room penthouse with a 75 m² terrace in Wilanów, asking 2,299,000 PLN (27,288 PLN/m²), the highest absolute price in the suite. Recorded Walk at 4.13% — a 31% price premium and an extreme 2,500 PLN czynsz. Sourced for a premium penthouse case.

Outer tier — a 61 m² 3-room new-build flat (Wawer Go 2.0, Falenica), asking 794,000 PLN (13,000 PLN/m²), among the cheapest per-m² in the suite. Recorded Borderline at 6.35% — a fair price and good yield offset by a transit-poor suburb. Wawer is a thin secondary market, so sale_comp_count_min is set to 10 (recorded 16) rather than the usual 20.

Outer tier, private seller — a 70 m² 3-room flat with parking on Osiedle Borki, Wawer, asking 960,000 PLN (13,675 PLN/m²). Recorded Borderline at 5.25% — an undisclosed czynsz and bus-only transit. Thin secondary market: sale_comp_count_min is set to 10 (recorded 14) rather than the usual 20. Risk substring 'transit' (replaced 'metro' after the red-team pass phrased the no-metro risk as 'bus-only transit, rail 3 km away').

Outer tier, private seller — a 37 m² 2-room flat with a garden on Osiedle Szafirowa Aleja, Białołęka, asking 600,000 PLN (16,216 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 4.8% — a 12.7% price premium for an outer suburb with no rail transit. Sourced for Białołęka coverage.

Outer tier — a 38 m² 2-room new-build (shell-finish, under construction) in Białołęka, asking 517,000 PLN (13,728 PLN/m²), the lowest absolute price in the suite. Recorded Borderline (leaning Walk) at 6.03% — construction-and-delivery risk, a supply overhang and a transit deficit. Sourced for a budget-tier new-build profile.

Central tier — a 26 m² studio in Powiśle, Śródmieście, to-renovate, asking 540,000 PLN (20,532 PLN/m²). Recorded Walk at 5.44% — an exceptionally high czynsz per m² and renovation capex undercut a strong central location. Sourced for a central studio profile and a cheap-for-central reference.

Inner-ring tier — a 58 m² 3-room off-plan ground-floor flat with a garden and a 28 m² terrace in Wola, asking 1,046,000 PLN (17,990 PLN/m²), ~20% below the comp median. Recorded Borderline at 5.6% — completion risk and a discount that needs explanation. Sourced for a second Wola profile alongside the 1959 reference case.
